compiled_state.title: As a developer, a regression suite verifies that sourcing the ticket library never leaks side effects into my shell
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Build a sourceability regression test suite that locks in the contract established in Story B: sourcing `ticket-lib-api.sh` must not bleed `set -euo pipefail`, `exit` from functions, `trap EXIT` leakage, `GIT_*` env mutations, or function-name shadowing into the caller's shell. Cover subshell isolation edge cases — functions that need local `set -e` or EXIT traps must wrap in `( ... )` without losing exit-status propagation. The core contract already landed in Story B; this story hardens it with a comprehensive regression suite so future library additions cannot silently regress the guarantee.

**Why**: SC4 (sourcing is side-effect-free) is a cross-cutting correctness guarantee. One-off validation in Story B is not enough; as Stories C and D add functions, subtle bleed could reintroduce. A regression suite that runs on every CI build is the durable defense.

**Scope**:
- IN: Regression test suite exercising every library function against a strict-mode caller (`set -euo pipefail`, `trap EXIT` preset) and asserting the caller's shell state is identical before and after; subshell-wrap coverage for any function that sets local `set -e` or installs a local trap; function-name collision detection (callers with pre-existing functions matching library names are not silently shadowed); `GIT_*` env-var preservation test across every covered op; test for `exit`-call avoidance (any library function that calls `exit` would terminate the caller — asserted absent).
- OUT: Cross-platform testing (Story F); performance benchmarks (Story G); new library ops.

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, a sourceability regression test suite exists and runs in CI, covering every library function defined in `ticket-lib-api.sh`
  ← Satisfies: "Sourcing the library is side-effect-free to the caller's shell: no `set -euo pipefail` propagation, no `exit` termination, no `trap EXIT` leakage, no mutation of `GIT_DIR`/`GIT_INDEX_FILE`/`GIT_WORK_TREE`/`GIT_COMMON_DIR` env vars"
- When this story is complete, sourcing the library from a strict-mode caller (`set -euo pipefail`, preset `trap EXIT`) does not abort the caller, does not replace the caller's `trap EXIT`, and does not mutate `GIT_*` env vars — verified by fixture tests
  ← Satisfies: "Sourcing the library is side-effect-free to the caller's shell"
- When this story is complete, any library function that locally uses `set -e`, `exit`-style termination, or a local `trap EXIT` is wrapped in a subshell `( ... )` without losing exit-status propagation to the caller — verified by subshell-wrap tests
  ← Satisfies: "Sourcing the library is side-effect-free to the caller's shell"
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Testing] Fixture test harness must itself run under strict mode so test flakes surface contract violations
- [Maintainability] Regression suite is the standing guard — any library addition must keep it green

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
